qa_pairs.question: What are the requirements for Co-Principal Investigators (Co-PIs) regarding their Canadian Common CV (CCV) when they are part of a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application with the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: All Co-PIs on a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application must have an updated Canadian Common CV (CCV) on file, meaning it must have been submitted within the last 90 days. The Academic Principal Investigator (PI) will not be able to submit their RAC application until this requirement is met for all Co-PIs.

qa_pairs.question: When an Academic Principal Investigator submits a new Canadian Common CV (CCV) for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application to the Alliance, how do they report publications enabled by computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: After submitting a new Canadian Common CV (CCV), the publications from the Alliance CCV template will appear in the 'CCV Publications Reporting' section on CCDB, initially surrounded by a red border. To submit the RAC application, the PI must provide an answer for all publications to remove these red borders. This can be done by checking individual publications, clicking 'Select all new' to mark all as enabled, or clicking 'Clear all new' if none were enabled. To mark a publication as *not* enabled by computational resources, the PI must click on it twice.

qa_pairs.question: What action is required from an Academic Principal Investigator if their Canadian Common CV (CCV) on file is older than 90 days when they are applying for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) with the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Academic Principal Investigator's Canadian Common CV (CCV) on file is older than 90 days, they are required to submit a new CCV with their Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application. This involves visiting the CCV website to make necessary changes to the Alliance CCV template, submitting it, and then entering the new CCV confirmation code on CCDB and clicking 'Update CCV'.
